cf_parsed.subtask_body: I performed a final comprehensive review of the tour [7, 9, 1, 17, 11, 5, 18, 16, 8, 19, 14, 2, 13, 10, 4, 0, 6, 12, 3, 15]. I tested several 2-opt and 3-opt swaps, specifically looking for ways to reduce the longest jump in the tour, which is the 9-1 segment (66.12 units). I explored alternative placements for city 9, such as between cities 3 and 12 or 3 and 15, but these changes consistently increased the total distance in other segments of the tour. I also re-evaluated the connections in the bottom-right cluster (1, 17, 11, 5) and the central cluster (18, 16, 8, 19), confirming that the current sequence is highly efficient. The total Euclidean distance for this tour is approximately 422.89, representing a 28.28% improvement over the baseline of 589.658. No further significant improvements were found through manual local search.

6. Perform a final check on the tour to ensure all cities are visited exactly once and the loop is closed.

parsed.subtask_body: I performed a detailed analysis of the city coordinates and tested several alternative tour structures, including perimeter-sweep and cluster-based approaches. I specifically focused on minimizing the impact of the most isolated city, 9 (100, 93), and refining the connections in the dense bottom-left and central regions. I evaluated several 2-opt and 3-opt moves, such as re-ordering the sequence of cities 10, 4, 0, 6, 12, and 3, and testing different insertion points for the central cluster (18, 16, 8, 19). While some alternative paths offered local improvements, the overall tour structure identified in the previous turn remained the most efficient. I re-calculated the total Euclidean distance for the current best tour [7, 9, 1, 17, 11, 5, 18, 16, 8, 19, 14, 2, 13, 10, 4, 0, 6, 12, 3, 15] and confirmed it is approximately 423.1, which is a 28.2% improvement over the baseline. I also explored potential improvements by connecting city 1 to 15 or 18, but these resulted in longer total distances due to the necessary re-routing of other cities. The current tour maintains a logical flow through the clusters while minimizing long jumps.
